title: Understanding Arcium
summary: A concise overview of Arcium as a confidential execution layer and why it matters across the rest of the atlas.

bodySections.title: Core Idea
bodySections.body: Arcium is the privacy layer of Web3: an execution environment where applications can process sensitive inputs without exposing them to a single operator or to the wider network.

bodySections.title: Why teams care
bodySections.body: Builders care because many useful workloads become worse when everything is public by default. Trading intent, model inputs, private user state, and sensitive business logic all lose value when they are exposed during execution.

bodySections.title: How to Use This Atlas
bodySections.body: Start with the ecosystem map if you want breadth, then use the encyclopedia routes to deepen the model.
